2606.22248.title: SamatNext v0.2-B: An Exploratory Study of RMS-Normalized Hybrid Decoders for Curriculum Retention in Small Code Models
2606.22248.paper_summary: Standard autoregressive Transformer decoders can often exhibit substantial forgetting under sequential fine-tuning on shifting curriculum distributions. This technical report evaluates SamatNext v0.2-B, an experimental 356M-parameter hybrid sequence decoder that alternates Differential-Attention-style layers with DeltaNet-inspired simplified linear-state mixer layers using RMS normalization and output scale calibration. We study the model under a controlled staged Python code curriculum and compare it with a parameter-matched Transformer baseline. In this setting, SamatNext v0.2-B achieves a 100.0% pass rate on the controlled Stage 5 holdout while retaining 98.8% of adjacent Stage 3 semantic behavior and reaching 12.0% on the Stage 2E early syntax holdout. The strongest Transformer baseline reaches 97.6% on Stage 5 but retains only 6.0% of Stage 3 behavior. Both architectures remain weak on long-horizon early-stage retention, so the result should be interpreted as evidence of an altered retention/plasticity tradeoff in this controlled setting, not as a general solution to catastrophic forgetting. Code, model specifications, evaluation scripts, and result tables are provided for independent verification.
2606.22248.paper_summary_zh: 標準自回歸Transformer解碼器在對不斷變化的課程分佈進行序列微調時，往往會顯示出顯著的遺忘。這份技術報告評估了SamatNext v0.2-B，這是一個實驗性的356M參數混合序列解碼器，它交替使用差異注意力風格層和受DeltaNet啟發的簡化線性狀態混合層，並採用RMS正規化和輸出比例校準。我們在一個受控的階段Python代碼課程下研究該模型，並將其與一個參數匹配的Transformer基線進行比較。在這種情況下，SamatNext v0.2-B在受控的第五階段保留測試中達到了100.0%的通過率，同時保留了98.8%的相鄰第三階段語義行為，並在第二階段E的早期語法保留測試中達到了12.0%。最強的Transformer基線在第五階段達到了97.6%，但僅保留了6.0%的第三階段行為。這兩種架構在長期早期階段的保留上仍然較弱，因此結果應被解釋為在這個受控環境中改變的保留/可塑性權衡的證據，而不是對災難性遺忘的普遍解決方案。提供了代碼、模型規格、評估腳本和結果表格以供獨立驗證。
2606.22248.author: Samat Zharassov et.al.
